retry_reason: This is a put/free wrapper; failure indicates a double-free or refcount underflow bug—retrying would cause further corruption.
contract_summary: This function releases references held by an ovs_conntrack_info structure. It puts the helper module reference (if helper exists) via nf_conntrack_helper_put (and additionally nf_nat_helper_put when NAT is enabled), destroys any timeout, and frees the conntrack template via nf_ct_tmpl_free. After this call, the ct_info must not be accessed again. Callers must ensure this function is called exactly once for each allocated ovs_conntrack_info, typically as part of cleanup when the action is no longer needed. No return value is provided—the caller cannot detect failure from this call, so any failure would be a kernel bug.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is releasing references (put) on conntrack helper and template, and freeing the template. The function is a dedicated cleanup wrapper that decrements refcounts and frees resources with minimal unrelated logic.

check.reason: The function calls nf_conntrack_helper_put(ct_info->helper) when ct_info->helper is non-NULL, which decrements the helper's reference count. The access path from parameter ct_info to the refcount field (ct_info.helper.refcnt.refs) matches the callee's path (helper.refcnt.refs) when extended from the function's own parameter type. The type chain segment count (4) equals the access path segment count (4), and all types are consistent with the struct definitions.
